title: Merge v0.1 hardening and tag v0.1-feasibility
description: After all release-hardening children are complete, run final quality gates, merge the approved branch into main, and create the v0.1-feasibility tag. Git commit, merge, tag, and push require explicit user authorization at execution time.
acceptance_criteria: All release child issues are closed; full tests and Beads preflight pass; worktree scope is reviewed; approved commits are merged into main without unrelated files; v0.1-feasibility tag is created and pushed; README points to the final report and reproducibility command.

title: Close and publish ScoutLens v0.1 feasibility release
description:
Finish the current feasibility spike as a reproducible, internally consistent v0.1 release before treating external validation as normal implementation work. This epic groups operational Beads hardening, report consistency, reproducibility packaging, the human code-license decision, and final merge/tag work.

## Success Criteria
- Beads hooks and remote sync are operational and documented.
- Final report, decisions, and checked-in artifacts agree.
- Versioned config, data manifest/checksums, and drift checks meet the reproducibility definition of done.
- Code-license/publication posture is explicitly decided.
- Tests and Beads preflight pass; approved changes are merged and tagged v0.1-feasibility.

title: Map Wyscout-to-StatsBomb feature compatibility and freeze comparison design
description: Before implementing a second provider pipeline, determine whether the existing ScoutLens feature concepts can be reproduced faithfully enough for an interpretable external replication. Separate a canonical common-feature comparison from optional provider-native secondary analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory all 32 Wyscout features as direct equivalent, defensible approximation, or unavailable in StatsBomb; document event taxonomy, coordinates, possession, lineup/minutes, identifier, and missingness differences; freeze the canonical shared feature set and provider-native secondary set; define normalization and eligibility rules; record a GO/REDESIGN/NO-GO decision before pipeline implementation.

title: Harden Beads setup and backlog graph
description: Operationalize the new Beads adoption before starting external-validation work: make bd reachable by Codex hooks, publish Dolt state, choose an explicit JSONL policy, add definitions of done to priority work, and correct scientific dependencies discovered in the first backlog review.
acceptance_criteria: bd is reachable from a fresh Codex process; refs/dolt/data exists on origin; issues.jsonl has an intentional tracked-or-ignored policy; P1/P2 issues pass template lint; the StatsBomb compatibility gate and recruitment/model dependency chain are represented; final bd lint/blocked/ready outputs are reviewed.

title: Build StatsBomb ingestion/validation/minutes pipeline
description: StatsBomb's event taxonomy, coordinate system, and file structure are not compatible with scoutlens.data.ingestion/validation/minutes as built for Wyscout. This is the equivalent of redoing SLS-005 through SLS-014's work for a new source (schema profiling, raw-data validation, relational key checks, minutes derivation + tests), not reusing the existing pipeline with new input files. Blocked on the license/match-count audit confirming a usable dataset first.
acceptance_criteria: Implement provider-scoped StatsBomb ingestion, validation, relational-key checks, lineup/minutes derivation, and processed outputs; document schema and provenance; add unit and integration tests for malformed or incomplete inputs; preserve all Wyscout behavior and keep both providers independently reproducible.

title: Re-run temporal stability + transfer-analysis experiments on StatsBomb 2015/16
description: Once the StatsBomb pipeline exists: rebuild the feature catalog/aggregation for the new schema, re-run the chronological-split retrieval experiment (Baselines A/B/C) and the transferred-players follow-up, and compare against the Wyscout 2017/18 domestic-league results to check generalization. This is the actual external-validation payoff of the epic.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare eligibility, splits, features, Baselines A/B/C, primary metrics, and interpretation thresholds; run the experiment on all approved leagues; report eligible and transferred counts before interpreting precision; compare effect direction and robustness with Wyscout without attributing differences solely to season; regenerate versioned artifacts and a limitations-aware report.

title: External replication on StatsBomb 2015/16 (four leagues)
description:
Highest-value remaining research epic. Replicate the Wyscout 2017/18 finding on an independent provider and season using StatsBomb 2015/16 across Premier League, La Liga, Serie A, and Ligue 1. Test whether the direction of the stability signal survives the joint provider-plus-season change and estimate transferred-player performance without assuming that one extra season will produce a large sample. This is external replication, not a clean isolated test of temporal generalization.

## Success Criteria
- Mini Gate 0 documents legal usability, exact coverage, and a GO/NO-GO decision.
- A cross-provider feature-compatibility design is frozen before pipeline implementation.
- The StatsBomb pipeline is validated and covered by tests without regressing Wyscout.
- Baselines A/B/C and the transferred-player analysis are rerun with predeclared metrics and reported as external replication.

title: StatsBomb 2015/16 license + match-count audit (mini Gate 0)
description: Before any acquisition code is written for the StatsBomb extension: run a full per-artifact license audit matching data-provenance.md's rigor (today's StatsBomb check was only surface-level), and confirm the exact per-competition match count directly against the downloaded files (today's verification was corroborating via file-size scaling, not exhaustive). Candidate scope per feasibility-report.md: StatsBomb 2015/16, Premier League + La Liga + Serie A + Ligue 1 (Bundesliga's 2015/16 is a small ~34-match subset and is excluded). Also re-verify that La Liga 2018/19 should NOT be used (confirmed Messi/Barcelona-focused subset, not a full season — see decisions-log.md D013).
acceptance_criteria: Document artifact-level access, use, redistribution, publication, attribution, and logo obligations; count matches directly from official files for all candidate leagues; confirm unique match IDs, date span, team coverage, and why Ligue 1 has 377 rather than 380 matches; record Bundesliga exclusion; publish an explicit GO/NO-GO Gate 0 decision with provenance links.
notes: Deliverable complete 2026-07-23 (docs/statsbomb-provenance.md + D014): all acceptance criteria met — per-clause license audit, exact match counts (380/380/380/377 + the 3 specific missing Ligue 1 fixtures identified), Bundesliga exclusion confirmed (Leverkusen-only), La Liga 2018/19 reconfirmed Messi-only, 1517/1517 events+lineups coverage, pinned to open-data commit b0bc9f2. Verdict: GO for non-commercial research replication. Close is gated by scoutlens-0sg.1 (v0.1 merge+tag) per the release-first ordering — close this as soon as that clears; no further work needed here.

title: Execute recruitment validation study
description: Run the approved recruitment-oriented validation protocol on externally replicated ScoutLens outputs. This is the first issue allowed to support or reject a recruitment usefulness claim; same-player retrieval alone remains insufficient.
acceptance_criteria: External replication and the written protocol are complete; candidate shortlists are generated from frozen data and settings; the evaluation is blinded where applicable; primary and secondary metrics plus uncertainty and inter-rater results are reported; failures and subgroup behavior are analyzed; conclude GO/REDESIGN/NO-GO for any recruitment claim.

title: Fix stale robustness-ablation claim in feasibility-report.md Limitation #10
description:
The final report still describes the A-only-vs-combined standardization-fit ablation as future work even though robustness-checks.md Check 1 already ran it and found no meaningful difference.

## Steps to Reproduce
Open feasibility-report.md Known Limitation #10 and compare it with robustness-checks.md Check 1 (MRR 0.2543 vs 0.2539).
acceptance_criteria: Known Limitation #10 states that the ablation was completed, links to robustness-checks.md Check 1, reports the no-meaningful-difference result accurately, and no longer presents it as future work; documentation checks pass.
close_reason: Fix applied: Limitation #10 in feasibility-report.md now cites robustness-checks.md Check 1's A-only vs A+B result (MRR 0.2543 vs 0.2539) instead of describing the ablation as still-open.

title: Decide code license for the repository
description:
Choose the license for ScoutLens source code independently of the dataset license. This is a human publication decision and must not be inferred by an agent.

## Rationale
A public flagship release needs explicit reuse terms; no license leaves default copyright and makes reuse ambiguous.

## Alternatives Considered
- MIT: simplest permissive option.
- Apache-2.0: permissive with explicit patent terms.
- No public code license / keep private: valid if the repository is not being released for reuse.

title: Versioned run-config + data-checksum manifest, and report-vs-artifact diff test
description: feasibility-report.md Recommended Next Experiment #6 and Known Limitation #14 (partially resolved 2026-07-22 — see decisions-log.md D013). Still open after D013's lightweight fix (git commit + timestamp only, injected via _run_metadata() in run_report.py/run_robustness.py/run_transfer_analysis.py): (1) an externally versioned config file — today's threshold/competition-set/seed are inlined constants in each run_*.py, not a separate file; (2) a run-manifest recording data checksums (not just the code commit) the numbers were produced from; (3) an automated test that diffs the report's published prose numbers against a freshly-generated artifact, so drift is caught automatically rather than by manual review (today's test_artifacts.py only checks the checked-in artifact against hardcoded expected values, not a fresh run).
acceptance_criteria: Move experiment constants into a versioned config; produce a run manifest containing git commit, UTC timestamp, seeds, environment, and checksums for every input dataset; make runners consume and emit this metadata; add a fresh-run report-versus-artifact drift test; document the reproduction command.
close_reason: D015: config/experiment.json (versioned params), _manifest per artifact (config hash, git commit, env versions, sha256 per input parquet), fresh-run drift test (SCOUTLENS_DRIFT=1) completing docs<->artifact<->fresh-run chain. Drift test caught real nondeterminism (transferred_pairs row order) on first run; fixed with sort + regression test. All 3 drift tests pass; 117 unit tests pass.

title: Design recruitment-claim validation protocol
description: Design the genuinely different methodology needed to test recruitment usefulness. This issue defines the protocol only and can proceed while external replication is being prepared; execution will be tracked separately and will depend on the external-validation result.
acceptance_criteria: Define the recruitment decision being evaluated, candidate/query sampling, blinded expert or downstream-task protocol, rubric, primary and secondary metrics, inter-rater treatment, minimum viable sample, analysis plan, failure criteria, data/privacy constraints, and a written execution checklist ready for review.
close_reason: Protocol designed and pre-registered: docs/recruitment-validation-protocol.md + D016. Blinded expert shortlist study, 40 role-stratified queries, 3 arms (B / role+minutes / random), anchored 1-5 rubric, Krippendorff alpha reliability gate, paired Wilcoxon primary, 3 pre-declared failure criteria, privacy/license constraints, execution checklist for scoutlens-h00.

title: Test whether added model complexity earns its place (learned representation / metric learning)
description: feasibility-report.md Recommended Next Experiment #3. Only after external validation and recruitment-methodology work: compare a learned representation, metric learning, or feature reweighting against Baseline B *and* Baseline C (not today's version of either) to check if the complexity earns its place, per the charter's 'complexity must earn its place' principle. Explicitly ordered last — the charter originally proposed this first, and it was deliberately reordered twice already (see decisions-log.md) because the robustness battery and transfer-analysis follow-up were higher-value and cheaper first.
acceptance_criteria: Freeze datasets, splits, and comparison baselines before training; compare learned representations or metric learning against current and externally replicated Baseline B plus relevant simple baselines; predefine minimum practical improvement; report uncertainty, compute/maintenance cost, failure cases, and a keep-or-reject decision.

title: Shrinkage / minimum-attempts floor for low-sample ratio features
description: Known Limitation #11: ratio features computed from very few attempts can be extreme and over-trusted — a player with 1 shot and 1 goal gets shot_conversion_pct = 1.0, the same numeric value as a striker who converted 20 of 20, with no attempt-count-based confidence weighting. No shrinkage or minimum-attempts floor was applied in the v0 catalog. Candidate fix: empirical Bayes shrinkage toward the population mean, weighted by attempt count, or an explicit minimum-attempts floor before a ratio feature is trusted/included.
acceptance_criteria: Choose and document either empirical-Bayes shrinkage or a minimum-attempt policy per affected ratio; add edge-case tests including 0 attempts and 1/1 success; preserve raw numerator/denominator information; rerun the retrieval evaluation and report whether rankings or conclusions materially change; keep or reject the transformation explicitly.
notes: 2026-07-23: user decision — this experiment changes the feature catalog and published Baseline B numbers, so it belongs to v0.2. Gated on scoutlens-0sg.1 (v0.1 merge+tag); the drift test (D015) will flag the deliberate number changes when it runs.

title: Cluster-aware bootstrap CI (queries aren't fully independent draws)
description: Known Limitation #12: the bootstrap treats queries as independent draws, though they share teams, leagues, and the same period-B candidate pool — some correlation between queries is plausible (e.g. two teammates' retrieval difficulty could move together). The reported effect size is likely large enough to survive a more conservative cluster-aware interval, but today's exact CI bounds should be read as approximate, not exact, for this reason. Worth implementing a cluster-aware (e.g. block/cluster bootstrap by team or league) CI as a check, not necessarily a replacement for the current method.
acceptance_criteria: Define justified clustering units; implement deterministic cluster-aware resampling with tests for alignment and reproducibility; report cluster counts and CI beside the current paired bootstrap; explain any material inference change and retain the simpler interval only if clearly labeled approximate.
close_reason: D018: bootstrap_mrr_delta_clustered implemented (deterministic, order-independent, raises on unmapped queries; 4 unit tests). Check 6 in robustness-checks.md: team-clustered CI [0.2044,0.2525] (~23% wider), league-clustered [0.1952,0.2510] — conclusion unchanged, delta far from zero. Limitation #12 annotated as checked; iid interval retained as published headline, labeled approximate.

title: Sequence-involvement feature family
description: Known Limitation #4: sequence involvement was cut from the v0 feature catalog entirely under the scope-priority rule and never built. Evaluate whether it's worth adding as a v1 feature family, given the per-feature-family ablation (robustness-checks.md Check 5) showing the current 8 families each contribute weakly-to-moderately and the combined model's power comes from breadth, not one dominant family.
acceptance_criteria: Specify sequence boundaries and all proposed involvement features; implement tested aggregation without temporal leakage; add the family to the ablation runner; compare standalone and incremental value against the frozen v0 catalog; record a keep/drop decision based on predefined practical gain.
notes: 2026-07-23: user decision — this experiment changes the feature catalog and published Baseline B numbers, so it belongs to v0.2. Gated on scoutlens-0sg.1 (v0.1 merge+tag); the drift test (D015) will flag the deliberate number changes when it runs.

title: Resolve 3,603 vs 4,299 players-count discrepancy vs. source paper
description: feasibility-report.md Recommended Next Experiment #5 and Known Limitation #6: the processed dataset has 3,603 players against the Pappalardo paper's stated 4,299 — still unexplained. Low cost, closes a standing question rather than carrying it forward indefinitely.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce both 3,603 and 4,299 counting definitions from primary sources and local data; identify exclusions, duplicates, competitions, or schema differences explaining the gap; add a validation or documentation check preventing future ambiguity; update the report with the resolved reconciliation or a bounded residual explanation.
close_reason: Fully reconciled (D017): paper's Table 1 per-competition values reproduced exactly (sum 4,362 = per-competition rostered counts); printed total 4,299 is an arithmetic error in the paper itself; distinct union 3,618 = 3,603 profiled + 15 unused-bench players (0 min, 0 events). Pinned by tests/data/test_player_counts.py; docs updated (data-provenance, feasibility-report x2).

title: Goalkeeper-specific feature family
description: feasibility-report.md Recommended Next Experiment #4 and Known Limitation #7: goalkeepers are the weakest-signal role, consistent with an outfield-action-heavy feature catalog. Add a goalkeeper-specific feature family (positioning, distribution under pressure, claim/punch tendencies — none currently captured). Independent of the second-season epic; can be done in parallel against the existing Wyscout dataset.
acceptance_criteria: Audit which goalkeeper concepts are actually observable in the available event schema; define only supportable features with denominators and missingness rules; add aggregation tests and goalkeeper-specific evaluation; compare against the frozen catalog; document unavailable positioning or pressure concepts rather than proxying them without evidence.
notes: 2026-07-23: user decision — this experiment changes the feature catalog and published Baseline B numbers, so it belongs to v0.2. Gated on scoutlens-0sg.1 (v0.1 merge+tag); the drift test (D015) will flag the deliberate number changes when it runs.
